seo.title: Compress PDF to 500 KB - Online upload helper
seo.description: Use a browser PDF compressor when an application requests a file near 500 KB, with clear reminders about quality and browser limits.

lead: Try to reduce a PDF for stricter upload forms while keeping readability checks front and center.

strings.ctaText: A 500 KB limit is tight for scans and image-heavy PDFs. Start with safe browser optimization, then decide whether source edits are needed.
strings.toolCardText: Run structural optimization locally in the browser and download the result for checking.
strings.stepsTitle: Prepare a PDF for a 500 KB limit
strings.stepsLead: A smaller target needs more careful review than a normal attachment.
strings.stepOneText: If possible, export a fresh PDF from the original document instead of compressing a screenshot-heavy scan.
strings.stepTwoText: Use the browser tool, then compare the new size with the 500 KB requirement.
strings.stepThreeText: Check that text, signatures, stamps, and page order remain acceptable before uploading.
strings.limitationsTitle: 500 KB is not reachable for every document
strings.limitationsText: The tool attempts optimization, but a PDF with many images or pages may need pages removed, image resolution reduced, or a different source export.
strings.limitationsItemOne: Scanned ID forms and photo PDFs often stay large because each page is an image.
strings.limitationsItemTwo: Mobile browsers can run out of memory before a useful result is created.
strings.limitationsItemThree: Very small files may already have little structure left to optimize.
strings.privacyTitle: The selected file is processed by your browser
strings.privacyText: Your browser reads the PDF to create the result. Site analytics and ads may still load as normal web requests.
strings.privacyItemOneText: Keep the page open until you save the result you need.
strings.privacyItemTwoText: For official or confidential files, use a desktop browser and follow your organization’s rules.
strings.relatedTitle: Clean the PDF before another attempt
strings.relatedLead: Removing pages or preparing upload targets can help when compression alone is not enough.
strings.relatedCompressText: Try safe optimization and review the output.
strings.relatedUploadText: Preview file size targets and readiness notes.
strings.relatedManageText: Delete blank or unnecessary pages before compressing again.
strings.faqOneQuestion: Why is 500 KB difficult for some PDFs?
strings.faqOneAnswer: Photos, scans, embedded fonts, and many pages can make the file larger than the target even after optimization.
strings.faqTwoQuestion: Does this replace editing the original document?
strings.faqTwoAnswer: No. If the result is still too large, editing or exporting the source document with smaller images is often more effective.
strings.faqThreeQuestion: Should I use this for application forms?
strings.faqThreeAnswer: You can use it as a preparation step, but reopen the result and confirm the receiving platform accepts the file.
